(1 comment)

Patch Set 3: -Code-Review

I mean I guess I disagree ?

I don't fully understand what is the usecase you are trying to have. --enable-client but no modules support? wahat are you trying to achieve with that?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: -Code-Review
> 
> I mean I guess I disagree ?
> 
> I don't fully understand what is the usecase you are trying to have. --enable-client but no modules support? wahat are you trying to achieve with that?

Ok, but if --enable-client is supposed to enforce --enable-modules then the --enable-client --disable-modules combo should be detected as illegal earlier on and an error with explaining text printed.

What I'm after is that for debian builds I build the dkms package only (I have a small patch for this in place locally), not the lustre-client-modules package (dkms is superior to that). Thus when building the rest of the packages it should have --disable-modules. I guess I should revisit this and change the rules file to have --disable-client --disable-modules --enable-utils instead then... but my guess is that it will still trip on that since the check is so that it only skips the kernel checks for enable_dist.

Does that make more sense?

I'll make some tests...

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3: -Code-Review
> > 
> > I mean I guess I disagree ?
> > 
> > I don't fully understand what is the usecase you are trying to have. --enable-client but no modules support? wahat are you trying to achieve with that?
> 
> Ok, but if --enable-client is supposed to enforce --enable-modules then the --enable-client --disable-modules combo should be detected as illegal earlier on and an error with explaining text printed.
> 
> What I'm after is that for debian builds I build the dkms package only (I have a small patch for this in place locally), not the lustre-client-modules package (dkms is superior to that). Thus when building the rest of the packages it should have --disable-modules. I guess I should revisit this and change the rules file to have --disable-client --disable-modules --enable-utils instead then... but my guess is that it will still trip on that since the check is so that it only skips the kernel checks for enable_dist.
> 
> Does that make more sense?
> 
> I'll make some tests...

Even using
./configure --enable-utils --disable-client --disable-server --disable-modules
fails on this.
With the patch it doesn't.

Patch Set 3:

--enable-client --disable-modules does not make sense indeed and is worthy of a help printed or error generated or both.

In my view --disable-modules on its own should automatically trigger --disable-client --disable-server

once you configure with --disable-client --enable-modules does it actually even build anything with your patch outside of userspace?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> --enable-client --disable-modules does not make sense indeed and is worthy of a help printed or error generated or both.
> 
> In my view --disable-modules on its own should automatically trigger --disable-client --disable-server
> 
> once you configure with --disable-client --enable-modules does it actually even build anything with your patch outside of userspace?

No it does matter for the userland tools. Some tools are for the server side only. Others are just client only. Do we want server user land tools for the native client? No.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> > 
> > --enable-client --disable-modules does not make sense indeed and is worthy of a help printed or error generated or both.
> > 
> > In my view --disable-modules on its own should automatically trigger --disable-client --disable-server
> > 
> > once you configure with --disable-client --enable-modules does it actually even build anything with your patch outside of userspace?
> 
> No it does matter for the userland tools. Some tools are for the server side only. Others are just client only. Do we want server user land tools for the native client? No.

Um, today there's no "native client". Also what "Server side only" tools do we have? mkfs and tune2fs? We'd have to redo our packaging to also split client and server side tools then so there's no confusion of "oh, I installed this lustre utils package but nothing works"?

But other than that I guess I see your point, just wonder if it actually makes sense to build client only package of tools?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 3:
> > > 
> > > --enable-client --disable-modules does not make sense indeed and is worthy of a help printed or error generated or both.
> > > 
> > > In my view --disable-modules on its own should automatically trigger --disable-client --disable-server
> > > 
> > > once you configure with --disable-client --enable-modules does it actually even build anything with your patch outside of userspace?
> > 
> > No it does matter for the userland tools. Some tools are for the server side only. Others are just client only. Do we want server user land tools for the native client? No.
> 
> Um, today there's no "native client". Also what "Server side only" tools do we have? mkfs and tune2fs? We'd have to redo our packaging to also split client and server side tools then so there's no confusion of "oh, I installed this lustre utils package but nothing works"?
> 
> But other than that I guess I see your point, just wonder if it actually makes sense to build client only package of tools?

Eventually we will have the ability to build server only rpms as well (LU-8837). In that case it will be opposite but same issue.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 38c013c04e42ff654750c2fa951a35090616cd30 by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-18660 utils: add long opts for 'lfs project'

commit.subject: LU-18684 config: don't run kernel checks with modules disabled
commit.message:
LU-18684 config: don't run kernel checks with modules disabled

Running:

configure --enable-client --disable-server --disable-modules

runs kernel checks. They should only ever be run with modules enabled.

Fixes: a13fc434c57f ("LU-17662 osd-zfs: Support for ZFS 2.2.3")
Signed-off-by: Ake Sandgren <ake.sandgren@hpc2n.umu.se>
Change-Id: I97277474e1db554c23bf2fa3f80ac3a5b745bded
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57921
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
